title: Cheapest Prop Firms In 2026: Challenge Fees Compared

meta_description: Compare the cheapest prop firms in 2026. Apex Trader Funding ($147/mo), Topstep ($165/mo), FTMO ($155/mo). See ratings, fees, and features side by side.
excerpt: Discover the cheapest prop firms in 2026 with side-by-side fee, rating, and feature comparisons for Apex, Topstep, and FTMO. Find your best value prop firm.
content:
Why Choose the Cheapest Prop Firms in 2026?

Prop trading firms have exploded in popularity, offering traders the chance to access significant capital for a fraction of what traditional brokers require. But with dozens of firms now competing for your attention, challenge fees and monthly costs can add up fast. In 2026, finding the cheapest prop firms is more important than ever—especially for beginners, budget-conscious traders, or anyone looking to maximize their profit potential without risking thousands upfront.

But cheap doesn’t always mean best. This guide compares the lowest-cost prop firms, breaking down their challenge fees, features, and pros/cons so you can make an informed decision.

Quick Comparison Table: Cheapest Prop Firms (2026)

Prop FirmRatingMonthly Fee / Challenge FeeBest ForKey Features
Apex Trader Funding4.3/5$147/moFutures, BeginnersOne-step eval, 100% of first $25K, No daily drawdown
Topstep4.2/5$165/moFutures, Disciplined TradersTrusted brand, 100% of first $5K, Risk management focus
FTMO4.5/5$155/mo (est. $550 one-time for $50K)Forex, Algo/EAs90% profit split, Free trial, Fee refund on payout

Apex Trader Funding Review 2026: The Budget Futures Option

Apex Trader Funding continues to dominate the budget-friendly futures prop firm space in 2026. With a monthly fee of just $147, it’s the lowest ongoing cost among major futures firms. Apex’s one-step evaluation is a huge plus for new traders who want to avoid the stress of multi-phase challenges.

  • Pros: Simple one-step evaluation, 100% of first $25,000 in profits, No daily drawdown limit, Frequent discounts and promos.
  • Cons: Futures only (no forex, stocks, or crypto), Trailing threshold rules can be confusing for beginners.

If you’re new to prop trading or want to keep your upfront costs low, Apex is a top choice. For more details, see our Apex Trader Funding review.

Topstep Review 2026: Trusted, But Is It Cheap?

Topstep remains one of the most established names in prop trading. At $165/month, it’s slightly pricier than Apex, but offers a robust trader development program and a strong reputation for payouts and support.

  • Pros: 100% profit split on first $5,000, Emphasis on risk management, Large trader community, Reliable support.
  • Cons: Futures only, Evaluation rules (like consistency requirements) can be restrictive, Slightly higher monthly fee.

Topstep is ideal for traders who value structure and education, even if it’s not the absolute cheapest. For a deeper dive, read our Topstep review.

FTMO Review 2026: Cheapest for Forex Traders?

FTMO is the go-to for forex, indices, and crypto traders seeking a global prop firm. Their monthly fee is $155 (for a $50K account, paid as a one-time challenge fee), making it competitive with futures firms. FTMO stands out for its 90% profit split and the ability to use EAs and algos across multiple platforms.

  • Pros: Industry-leading 90% profit split, Free trial with unlimited retakes, Challenge fee refunded on first payout, Multi-asset support.
  • Cons: High failure rate (85-95%), Non-refundable fees can add up if you fail multiple times, Strict risk rules.

For disciplined forex traders, FTMO offers the best value—especially if you can pass the challenge on your first or second try. See our full FTMO review for more.

Hidden Costs and Fee Structures: What to Watch For

When comparing the cheapest prop firms, don’t just look at the headline price. Here’s what to consider:

  • Monthly vs. One-Time Fees: Apex and Topstep charge monthly, so costs add up if you take longer to pass. FTMO charges a one-time fee per challenge, but repeated failures can quickly exceed monthly models.
  • Refund Policies: FTMO refunds your challenge fee after your first payout. Apex and Topstep do not refund monthly fees, but offer discounts and promos.
  • Profit Splits: Apex and Topstep offer 100% on the first $25K/$5K, then 80-90%. FTMO offers 90% from the start.
  • Retake/Retry Options: FTMO’s free trial and unlimited retakes can save you money if you need practice. Apex and Topstep require a new subscription or fee for each attempt.

Cheapest Prop Firms vs. Competitors in 2026

How do these budget-friendly firms compare to others in the market? Apex, Topstep, and FTMO consistently rank among the lowest-cost options for their respective asset classes. However, new firms like AquaFunded, Blue Guardian, and BrightFunded are emerging with aggressive pricing and unique features.

For a detailed head-to-head, check out our Apex Trader Funding vs AquaFunded comparison or see how Apex stacks up against Blue Guardian and BrightFunded.

Which Cheapest Prop Firm Should You Choose?

Choosing the right prop firm in 2026 depends on your trading style, preferred markets, and budget:

  • Futures Traders: Apex Trader Funding is the cheapest and simplest for most, but Topstep offers more structure and support if you value education.
  • Forex/CFD Traders: FTMO is the clear winner for low-cost, high-payout forex funding—just be prepared for strict rules and high failure rates.
  • Beginners: Apex’s one-step evaluation and frequent discounts make it the lowest-risk entry point for new futures traders.
  • Algo/EAs: FTMO’s multi-platform support and generous profit split are ideal for automated traders.

Final Recommendation: Cheapest Prop Firms for 2026

In 2026, the cheapest prop firms aren’t just about the lowest fee—they’re about value, transparency, and your ability to pass the challenge. Here’s our bottom line:

  • Best Overall for Futures: Apex Trader Funding ($147/mo) — unbeatable for cost and simplicity.
  • Best for Forex/CFDs: FTMO ($155/mo or $550 one-time for $50K) — top profit split, global access.
  • Best for Structure & Support: Topstep ($165/mo) — trusted, educational, but slightly pricier.

Always read the fine print, consider your trading style, and use free trials or discounts where possible. For more in-depth comparisons and reviews, explore our full prop firm directory and comparison tools.
